description: Restaurant dining content showcasing food items with prices in SAR, location tags, and detailed order breakdowns targeting Riyadh's food-focused audience.
structural_recipe: Post high-quality food photography with: (1) Restaurant name and cuisine type with flag emoji, (2) Location tag with @ mentions to restaurant/food review accounts, (3) Itemized order list with dish names in Arabic and English, (4) Prices in SAR with ريال symbol, (5) Star/emoji ratings for recommended items, (6) Casual Arabic commentary praising taste quality using intensifiers like 'مرررره', 'لذيذ', 'يمي'
why_it_works: Saudi diners rely on peer recommendations and detailed pricing/item information before dining. This pattern combines visual appeal with practical data (prices, locations, dish names), reducing purchase friction. Bilingual captions reach broader audiences. Food review account tagging creates community engagement and provides social proof through aggregated recommendations.
transplantation_caveats: Price sensitivity varies by neighborhood—content performs better with transparent SAR pricing
Must include specific location tags (@rest.riyadh style accounts) to drive foot traffic and credibility
Bilingual Arabic-English format is essential; Arabic-only or English-only reduces engagement
Halal compliance implicit but unspoken—avoid non-halal cuisines without explicit mention
Peak engagement times align with Saudi meal times (lunch 12-2pm, dinner 8-10pm KSA time)
cultural_constraints: Avoid alcohol references or wine pairings
Gender-mixed dining imagery acceptable but group dining (family/friends) preferred over solo dining
Formal restaurant settings perform better than casual/street food contexts in premium segments
